Aswath Damodaran said the selloff reflected recalibration, not panic. Rising inflation expectations, higher bond yields and modest risk repricing drove equities lower amid continued uncertainty over the conflict’s trajectory.

title: Sebi considers bringing back open market buybacks via stock exchange route
summary: Sebi is looking at bringing back open market share buybacks via stock exchanges. This method was stopped earlier due to transparency and price discovery worries. Companies could soon have this as another option alongside tender offers. The regulator aims to improve capital returns for firms. This move could make capital allocation more dynamic for listed companies.

summary: Following Trump's remarks on Iran, US stock futures plummeted as he signaled no imminent end to military operations. Crude oil prices surged, with Brent crude experiencing significant gains, reflecting concerns over supply disruptions due to the conflict.

title: Sebi rethinks open market buyback ban as tax changes level playing field
summary: Under the old tax system, companies paid tax on buybacks, and shareholders who sold shares in the buyback did not pay tax on their gains. This meant only those who managed to sell benefited, while others did not. Sebi felt this created inequality.
